programme.title: Herbal Symphony
programme.desc: . Japan’s oldest private herb garden in Uda, Nara Prefecture has a 300-year history. To carry this legacy into the future, knowledge of medicinal herbs is shared with younger generations.

programme.title: Trails to Oishii Tokyo 10min.
programme.desc: Eggplants nasu take the spotlight. Enjoy juicy mizu-nasu and melt in your mouth Kamo nasu. From traditional shojin ryori to sweets, discover the surprising range of Japanese eggplants.

programme.title: Medical Frontiers
programme.desc: s. Using this data, Kyushu University identified two markers linked to early dementia. We present the latest findings. Erica Angyal, Maxwell Powers.
programme.category: 2015. Hisayama Town in southern Japan has held health checkups since the

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: My Street Piano
programme.desc: . Pianos are placed in airports, stations and street corners around the world for anyone to play. Join us as we unfold life stories of each piano player and watch them through fixed camera lenses.

programme.title: Activate Your Japanese!
programme.desc: . Learn how to make the most of your Japanese skills. Watch our drama that features useful communication strategies, our snappy videos about onomatopoeia, and our documentaries on "senpai" in Japan.

programme.title: Dig More Japan
programme.desc: . NHK WORLD-JAPAN features all kinds of programs to capture kids' imaginations and foster creativity. Let's dig a little deeper.

programme.title: Dining with the Chef mini
programme.desc: . Featured recipe: Cherry Blossom-shaped Crab Rice/ Learn about Japanese home cooking with Master Chef Saito, based on traditional Japanese cooking techniques in five minutes.

programme.title: Sumopedia
programme.desc: . Sumopedia enriches your sumo experience with insights into the sport’s techniques, traditions and legendary wrestlers. While the rules are simple, this iconic Japanese sport has far more depth than meets the eye.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Time-lapse Journey
programme.desc: . The world comes alive in a different way after sunset, with bright city lights or the glow of a 24-hour industrial complex. See how the scenery changes through the night with time-lapse photography.

programme.title: Journeys In Japan
programme.desc: , Episode 569. Colleen experiences the cosmopolitan port city Nagasaki during Obon, when people welcome home deceased family members with fireworks. And she sees other ways China has influenced the city. Bill Sullivan, Kyle Card, Michael Keida, Dean Newcombe, James Lambiasi, Natsumi Nagaoka, Atsushi Takase, Yosuke Hosoi.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Century Signboards
programme.desc: . Nori tsukudani, a popular topping in rice-loving Japan. Made with local nori from Tokyo Bay, it's a taste and tradition preserved for over 100 years.

programme.title: MATSURI: The Heartbeat of Japan
programme.desc: . Higashinada Danjiri involves a parade of decorated floats, pulled by teams of veterans and ridden by young performers that lean out over the crowd. 32 districts take part with their own float.

programme.title: Dining With The Chef
programme.desc: . Learn how to cook tempura at home. Chef Saito demonstrates the techniques needed to make crispy tempura using mountain vegetables of spring. Tatsuo Saito, Rika Yukimasa, Yu Hayami, Patrick Harlan.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Herbal Symphony
programme.desc: , Episode 2, Season 1. In Uda, discover Japan's oldest private medicinal herb garden, where the natural landscape from 300 years ago is preserved in a botanical time capsule, a sanctuary of the local culture and history.

programme.title: Trails to Oishii Tokyo 10min.
programme.desc: Kiriboshi daikon-daikon radish that's cut and sun-dried to preserve it. Both tasty and nutritious, it's gaining traction as a health food. Discover more about its unique texture and versatility.

programme.title: Document 72 Hours
programme.desc: . Locals have slurped down bowls of noodles at a 24-hour ramen restaurant in Fukuoka for five decades. For three days, we asked customers why this simple ramen is more than just a cheap meal. Masako Nishijima.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: My Street Piano
programme.desc: . Shimanto is a nature-rich town in Kochi Prefecture. A piano is placed on a park's outdoor stage, and people stop to play from their heart.

programme.title: Activate Your Japanese!
programme.desc: . Learn how to make the most of your Japanese skills. Watch our drama that features useful communication strategies, our snappy videos about onomatopoeia, and our documentaries on "senpai" in Japan.

programme.title: BOSAI: Science that Can Save Your Life
programme.desc: . The historic city of Kyoto faces threats of landslides and post-earthquake fires. What modern systems defend this ancient city from natural disasters.

programme.title: Sumopedia
programme.desc: . Sumopedia enriches your sumo experience with insights into the sport’s techniques, traditions and legendary wrestlers. While the rules are simple, this iconic Japanese sport has far more depth than meets the eye.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: The World Heritage Sites in Japan
programme.desc: . From natural landscapes to historic architecture representing centuries of cultural development, Japan's World Heritage sites offer a wide range of glimpses into history and the beauty of nature.

programme.title: Japan from Above: Up Close
programme.desc: . This program takes you on an aerial journey across 21st century Japan. Enjoy the bird's-eye view of unique landscapes as well as intimate portraits of the people who inhabit the archipelago.

programme.title: Medical Frontiers
programme.desc: s. Using this data, Kyushu University identified two markers linked to early dementia. We present the latest findings. Erica Angyal, Maxwell Powers.
programme.category: 2015. Hisayama Town in southern Japan has held health checkups since the

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Cycle Around Japan
programme.desc: . A series showcasing the stunning landscapes featured in Cycle Around Japan. This time: the rural surroundings of historic Kyoto, water transportation, and a nighttime ride through bustling Osaka. Zac Reynolds.

programme.title: Darwin's Amazing Animals
programme.desc: , Episode 6, Season 2. It's the world's fastest land animal. But that rarely helps in Africa's bush where the cheetah thrives. Let's discover some of its amazing hunting techniques, including what looks like teleporting!. Bill Sullivan, Jeff Manning, Tetsunori Kikuchi, Masahiro Hayakawa, Koichi Okumura.

programme.title: Time-lapse Journey
programme.desc: , Episode 4, Season 2. The Amakusa region has over 120 islands and is home to one of the places where a unique form of Christianity developed. See Amakusa's historic sights and natural beauty through time-lapse photography.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Century Signboards
programme.desc: , Episode 8, Season 1. An entertainment hall home to rakugo comic stories and more for over 100 years. A place to relax and forget your cares, food and drink are welcome.

programme.title: Satoyama Kitchen Spring
programme.desc: At the Satoyama Kitchen guesthouse in Hida Takayama, chef Nobuko, her cameraman husband Makoto and their cat Tama take us on a tasty journey through local cuisine, served with a side of seasonal scenery.

programme.title: Direct Talk
programme.desc: , Ibuki became the first Japanese winner of the world's most prestigious magic competition. He talks about his swift rise to success, and explains how he comes up with original tricks. Gene Otani.

programme.title: Magical Japanese
programme.desc: , Episode 31, Season 1. The Japanese language is rich in words and expressions influenced by nature, history, and culture. This episode looks at words related to bow and arrow. Explore the unique culture behind them.

programme.title: Easy Japanese For Work
programme.desc: , Episode 19. #19 I'd like a pair of gloves. / Tell store clerks what you want by putting "ga hoshiin desu ga" after the name of the item.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Somewhere Street- A Little Stroll
programme.desc: A 15-minute spin-off of the "Somewhere Street" series, "A Short Stroll" explores cities through a traveler's eyes.Today we visit Vancouver, a seaport Canadian city.

programme.title: Dining With The Chef
programme.desc: . Learn how to cook tempura at home. Chef Saito demonstrates the techniques needed to make crispy tempura using mountain vegetables of spring. Tatsuo Saito, Rika Yukimasa, Yu Hayami, Patrick Harlan.

programme.title: Time-lapse Journey
programme.desc: . The world comes alive in a different way after sunset, with bright city lights or the glow of a 24-hour industrial complex. See how the scenery changes through the night with time-lapse photography.

programme.desc: . Locals have slurped down bowls of noodles at a 24-hour ramen restaurant in Fukuoka for five decades. For three days, we asked customers why this simple ramen is more than just a cheap meal. Masako Nishijima.

programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Century Signboards
programme.desc: . Nori tsukudani, a popular topping in rice-loving Japan. Made with local nori from Tokyo Bay, it's a taste and tradition preserved for over 100 years.

programme.title: MATSURI: The Heartbeat of Japan
programme.desc: . Gojinjo Taiko is a wild, powerful performance by masked drummers. It originated 400 years ago, when villagers disguised themselves as oni to scare away a warlord's army.

programme.title: Science View
programme.desc: . Squid and cuttlefish use camouflage not just to hide but to communicate. Vanguard Researchers explores the mechanism behind plant immunity. Tomoko Tashiro, Naoko Yamazaki, Michelle Yamamoto, Yoichi Sato, Tomoko Tina Kimura, Rena Yamada, Ross Mihara, Rachel Walzer.

programme.title: Century Signboards
programme.desc: , Episode 1, Season 1. Tokyo's oldest izakaya, with its traditional fare and lucky cats, surviving war and pandemic, has been a place to feel at ease for over 100 years.

programme.title: One in Every Japanese
programme.desc: . We interview women who use matchmaking apps, including Manami, who uses multiple apps to find a perfect partner.
programme.category: Episode 2. 1 in 10 Japanese got married through online spouse-hunting services in

programme.title: The Masterpieces of Ukiyo-e producer Tsutaju
programme.desc: . This miniseries presents the masterpieces of Tsutaju, a brilliant ukiyo-e producer of the Edo period. This episode spotlights ukiyo-e artist Sharaku's debut work "The Actor Otani Oniji III as Edobei.

programme.title: Direct Talk
programme.desc: , Ibuki became the first Japanese winner of the world's most prestigious magic competition. He talks about his swift rise to success, and explains how he comes up with original tricks. Gene Otani.

programme.title: DOSUKOI Sumo Salon 5 minutes
programme.desc: . This technique has been used in clutch moments in some historic bouts. We learn how it's different from the overarm throw and hear secrets of how it's done from the masters.

programme.title: One in Every Japanese
programme.desc: . We interview women who use matchmaking apps, including Manami, who uses multiple apps to find a perfect partner.

programme.title: Ukiyoe Edo-life
programme.desc: earthquake, an angry guardian deity set off on a quest to capture those considered responsible.
programme.category: 2018. Japan is a seismically active land; the Edo period was no exception. In the aftermath of a major

programme.title: Stoking the Fire of Hope
programme.desc: In recent years, night high schools in Japan are admitting more students with international roots. We look at a school in Tokyo and a teacher dedicated to guiding his students toward a better future.

programme.title: Document 72 Hours
programme.desc: . Locals have slurped down bowls of noodles at a 24-hour ramen restaurant in Fukuoka for five decades. For three days, we asked customers why this simple ramen is more than just a cheap meal. Masako Nishijima.

programme.desc: earthquake. A piano is placed in a shopping arcade for a limited period, and people stop to play from their heart.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia, and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Century Signboards
programme.desc: , Episode 7, Season 1. A sweet drink loved since the samurai days, amazake is made from rice fermented with koji mold. A secret family recipe preserved for over 100 years.

programme.title: MATSURI: The Heartbeat of Japan
programme.desc: , Episode 4, Season 1. Yoshida Ryusei, an October matsuri, features rockets called ryusei that are made by local teams and carry community prayers high in the sky. Team members pour heart and soul into their efforts.

programme.title: Dining With The Chef
programme.desc: . Learn how to cook tempura at home. Chef Saito demonstrates the techniques needed to make crispy tempura using mountain vegetables of spring. Tatsuo Saito, Rika Yukimasa, Yu Hayami, Patrick Harlan.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia, and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Herbal Symphony
programme.desc: , Episode 2, Season 1. In Uda, discover Japan's oldest private medicinal herb garden, where the natural landscape from 300 years ago is preserved in a botanical time capsule, a sanctuary of the local culture and history.

programme.title: Trails to Oishii Tokyo 10min.
programme.desc: Peaches take the spotlight. From June to October, new varieties appear every two weeks, letting people enjoy them for months. Big, sweet, and juicy discover the charm of Japan's finest peaches.

programme.title: Activate Your Japanese!
programme.desc: . Learn how to make the most of your Japanese skills. Watch our drama that features useful communication strategies, our snappy videos about onomatopoeia, and our documentaries on "senpai" in Japan.

programme.title: Dig More Japan
programme.desc: . NHK WORLD-JAPAN features all kinds of programs to capture kids' imaginations and foster creativity. Let's dig a little deeper.

programme.title: Dining with the Chef mini
programme.desc: . Featured recipe: Cherry Blossom-shaped Crab Rice/ Learn about Japanese home cooking with Master Chef Saito, based on traditional Japanese cooking techniques in five minutes.

programme.title: Sumopedia
programme.desc: . Sumopedia enriches your sumo experience with insights into the sport’s techniques, traditions and legendary wrestlers. While the rules are simple, this iconic Japanese sport has far more depth than meets the eye.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia, and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: My Street Piano
programme.desc: . Tokyo's Arakawa Ward has a shopping street with over 100 years of history. There sits a piano, which passersby love to visit and play from the heart.

programme.title: Dive in Tokyo
programme.desc: . Tokyo: where both tradition and the latest trends coexist. Join us on a journey to historic old towns and encounter many fun surprises along the way.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia, and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: The World Heritage Sites in Japan
programme.desc: . From natural landscapes to historic architecture representing centuries of cultural development, Japan's World Heritage sites offer a wide range of glimpses into history and the beauty of nature.

programme.title: Japan from Above: Up Close
programme.desc: . This program takes you on an aerial journey across 21st century Japan. Enjoy the bird's-eye view of unique landscapes as well as intimate portraits of the people who inhabit the archipelago.

programme.title: Direct Talk
programme.desc: , Episode 269. Sugiura Mitsuyoshi is gaining attention both at home and abroad for transforming old kimonos into new fashion statements. He talks about his unique efforts to revitalize a traditional industry. Gene Otani.

programme.title: Japan's Top Inventions
programme.desc: , Episode 58. Discover the stories behind renowned Japanese products alongside our reporter. This time: laser flaw inspection systems. Peter Von Gomm, Yuki Rhinehart, Jason David Danielson, Koki Ando, Toru Iwatani, Shigetaka Kurita, Yoshihiko Akimoto.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Century Signboards
programme.desc: , Episode 1, Season 1. Tokyo's oldest izakaya, with its traditional fare and lucky cats, surviving war and pandemic, has been a place to feel at ease for over 100 years.

programme.title: MATSURI: The Heartbeat of Japan
programme.desc: . A week into the new year, giant torches are lit in a shrine precinct. The shadows they create enable a demon-oni-to go to a river and cleanse people of troubles. This is Oniyo: the night of the oni.

programme.title: Frontrunners
programme.desc: . Taira Yuiko promotes composting of food waste to create a sustainable cycle. She works with an NGO in Mongolia to form a local nutrient cycle, enriching the soil and teaching composting techniques.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Herbal Symphony
programme.desc: , Episode 1, Season 1. As spring arrives, Japan's oldest private herb garden in Uda, Nara Prefecture, bursts into life. This episode tells the story of the people who follow in the footsteps of their ancestors to preserve these herbs.

programme.title: Trails to Oishii Tokyo 10min.
programme.desc: Episode 3, Season 1. Unique Japanese egg dishes, such as raw egg over rice, are popular with international visitors. Visit farms that produce eggs safe to eat raw, and enjoy a variety of dishes at a long-established restaurant in Kyoto.

programme.title: Science View
programme.desc: . Squid and cuttlefish use camouflage not just to hide but to communicate. Vanguard Researchers explores the mechanism behind plant immunity. Tomoko Tashiro, Naoko Yamazaki, Michelle Yamamoto, Yoichi Sato, Tomoko Tina Kimura, Rena Yamada, Ross Mihara, Rachel Walzer.

programme.title: My Street Piano
programme.desc: . Shibuya is one of Tokyo's largest cities. A piano is placed near a ticket gate at the station, and people stop to play from their heart.

programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Century Signboards
programme.desc: , Episode 4, Season 1. Edo Kiriko techniques have been passed down from artisan to artisan for over 100 years. Today, more women pursuing the craft are creating popular new designs.

programme.title: MATSURI: The Heartbeat of Japan
programme.desc: . At the Niihama Taiko Matsuri, teams of around 150 people carry taikodai: three-tonne parade floats housing a huge taiko drum. They compete to give the most powerful synchronized performance.

programme.title: Direct Talk
programme.desc: , Episode 269. Sugiura Mitsuyoshi is gaining attention both at home and abroad for transforming old kimonos into new fashion statements. He talks about his unique efforts to revitalize a traditional industry. Gene Otani.

programme.title: Japan's Top Inventions
programme.desc: , Episode 58. Discover the stories behind renowned Japanese products alongside our reporter. This time: laser flaw inspection systems. Peter Von Gomm, Yuki Rhinehart, Jason David Danielson, Koki Ando, Toru Iwatani, Shigetaka Kurita, Yoshihiko Akimoto.

programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Herbal Symphony
programme.desc: , Episode 3, Season 1. In Uda, discover Japan's oldest private medicinal herb garden, where the natural landscape from 300 years ago is preserved in a botanical time capsule, a sanctuary of the local culture and history.

programme.title: Trails to Oishii Tokyo 10min.
programme.desc: Wagyu captivates food lovers worldwide with fine marbling, melt-in-the-mouth texture, and rich aroma. Join us as we explore strict cattle management and the dishes that highlight wagyu's appeal.

programme.title: Frontrunners
programme.desc: . Taira Yuiko promotes composting of food waste to create a sustainable cycle. She works with an NGO in Mongolia to form a local nutrient cycle, enriching the soil and teaching composting techniques.

programme.desc: . This program takes you on an aerial journey across 21st century Japan. Enjoy the bird's-eye view of unique landscapes as well as intimate portraits of the people who inhabit the archipelago.

programme.title: Science View
programme.desc: . Squid and cuttlefish use camouflage not just to hide but to communicate. Vanguard Researchers explores the mechanism behind plant immunity. Tomoko Tashiro, Naoko Yamazaki, Michelle Yamamoto, Yoichi Sato, Tomoko Tina Kimura, Rena Yamada, Ross Mihara, Rachel Walzer.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Cycle Around Japan
programme.desc: , Episode 2, Season 1. A series showcasing the stunning landscapes featured in Cycle Around Japan. This time: the beautiful islands of Yamaguchi, and the historical scenery of the Izumo Highway. Zac Reynolds.

programme.title: BOSAI: Science that Can Save Your Life
programme.desc: . The historic city of Kyoto faces threats of landslides and post-earthquake fires. What modern systems defend this ancient city from natural disasters.

programme.title: IMPACTS: Climates Change the World
programme.desc: , Episode 26, Season 1. Global warming leads to widespread famine. By the end of the century, it is predicted that yields of maize, soybeans, and wheat may fall by 30-40%. We examine the scientific data on this global issue.

programme.title: Time-lapse Japan
programme.desc: . Since ancient times, safflowers grown in Yonezawa have been used to dye fabric a deep red color associated with the sun. Experience its mysterious, vivid beauty through time-lapse footage.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Century Signboards
programme.desc: , Episode 9, Season 1. Sweet-bean-paste-filled taiyaki in the auspicious shape of a seabream or Tai. The Japanese have loved these sweet treats for over 100 years.

programme.title: Satoyama Kitchen Spring
programme.desc: At the Satoyama Kitchen guesthouse in Hida Takayama, chef Nobuko, her cameraman husband Makoto and their cat Tama take us on a tasty journey of local cuisine served with a side of seasonal scenery.

programme.title: Direct Talk
programme.desc: . He talks about the potential of physical expression. Gene Otani.
programme.category: Episode 255. Leading Japanese dancer and choreographer Kondo Ryohei served as director of the opening ceremony for the Tokyo Deaflympics in November

programme.title: DOSUKOI Sumo Salon 5 minutes
programme.desc: . 33 wins over 45 bouts in 3 tournaments is one benchmark for promotion to Ozeki, but some have advanced with just 28 wins while others failed despite 35. Explore the stories behind these promotions.

programme.title: One in Every Japanese
programme.desc: 1 in 30 Japanese suffer from mental illness. We follow a woman who finds it hard to do simple tasks due to her illness, as she goes to rehabilitation for returning to work.

programme.title: Ukiyoe Edo-life
programme.desc: earthquake, an angry guardian deity set off on a quest to capture those considered responsible.
programme.category: 2018. Japan is a seismically active land; the Edo period was no exception. In the aftermath of a major

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Somewhere Street A Short Stroll
programme.desc: . A 15-minute spin-off of the "Somewhere Street" series, "A Short Stroll" explores cities around the world. This episode invites people who love hills to explore Genoa, Italy.

programme.title: Direct Talk
programme.desc: , Episode 269. Sugiura Mitsuyoshi is gaining attention both at home and abroad for transforming old kimonos into new fashion statements. He talks about his unique efforts to revitalize a traditional industry. Gene Otani.

programme.title: Japan's Top Inventions
programme.desc: , Episode 58. Discover the stories behind renowned Japanese products alongside our reporter. This time: laser flaw inspection systems. Peter Von Gomm, Yuki Rhinehart, Jason David Danielson, Koki Ando, Toru Iwatani, Shigetaka Kurita, Yoshihiko Akimoto.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: The World Heritage Sites in Japan
programme.desc: . From natural landscapes to historic architecture representing centuries of cultural development, Japan's World Heritage sites offer a wide range of glimpses into history and the beauty of nature.

programme.title: Japan from Above: Up Close
programme.desc: . This program takes you on an aerial journey across 21st century Japan. Enjoy the bird's-eye view of unique landscapes as well as intimate portraits of the people who inhabit the archipelago.

programme.title: Frontrunners
programme.desc: . Taira Yuiko promotes composting of food waste to create a sustainable cycle. She works with an NGO in Mongolia to form a local nutrient cycle, enriching the soil and teaching composting techniques.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Century Signboards
programme.desc: , Episode 4, Season 1. Edo Kiriko techniques have been passed down from artisan to artisan for over 100 years. Today, more women pursuing the craft are creating popular new designs.

programme.title: MATSURI: The Heartbeat of Japan
programme.desc: , Episode 4, Season 1. Yoshida Ryusei, an October matsuri, features rockets called ryusei that are made by local teams and carry community prayers high in the sky. Team members pour heart and soul into their efforts.

programme.title: Trails to Oishii Tokyo
programme.desc: . Wakame, one of Japan's most popular types of seaweed, takes us to Sanriku in Miyagi Prefecture. Experience harvesting giant wakame and explore research advancing environmental protection.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Herbal Symphony
programme.desc: , Episode 1, Season 1. As spring arrives, Japan's oldest private herb garden in Uda, Nara Prefecture, bursts into life. This episode tells the story of the people who follow in the footsteps of their ancestors to preserve these herbs.

programme.title: Trails to Oishii Tokyo 10min.
programme.desc: Wagyu captivates food lovers worldwide with fine marbling, melt-in-the-mouth texture, and rich aroma. Join us as we explore strict cattle management and the dishes that highlight wagyu's appeal.

programme.title: Dive in Tokyo
programme.desc: . Tokyo: where both tradition and the latest trends coexist. Join us on a journey to historic old towns and encounter many fun surprises along the way.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: My Street Piano
programme.desc: , Episode 2, Season 3. Kanazawa City, Ishikawa Prefecture, is a known castle town. In one commercial area sits a piano, which passersby love to visit and play from the heart.

programme.title: The Garden Way
programme.desc: No Desription Available.

programme.title: Sumopedia
programme.desc: . Sumopedia enriches your sumo experience with insights into the sport’s techniques, traditions and legendary wrestlers. While the rules are simple, this iconic Japanese sport has far more depth than meets the eye.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: The World Heritage Sites in Japan
programme.desc: . From natural landscapes to historic architecture representing centuries of cultural development, Japan's World Heritage sites offer a wide range of glimpses into history and the beauty of nature.

programme.title: Japan from Above: Up Close
programme.desc: . This program takes you on an aerial journey across 21st century Japan. Enjoy the bird's-eye view of unique landscapes as well as intimate portraits of the people who inhabit the archipelago.

programme.title: Direct Talk
programme.desc: , Episode 269. Sugiura Mitsuyoshi is gaining attention both at home and abroad for transforming old kimonos into new fashion statements. He talks about his unique efforts to revitalize a traditional industry. Gene Otani.

programme.desc: , Episode 58. Discover the stories behind renowned Japanese products alongside our reporter. This time: laser flaw inspection systems. Peter Von Gomm, Yuki Rhinehart, Jason David Danielson, Koki Ando, Toru Iwatani, Shigetaka Kurita, Yoshihiko Akimoto.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Century Signboards
programme.desc: . Using traditional wooden barrels, a soy sauce brewer developed its own original flavor, breathing new life into the world of Japanese soy sauce.

programme.title: One in Every Japanese
programme.desc: Japanese have experience as a lay judge in court cases. We witness a networking event for former lay judges, where they discuss unique experiences. What does it feel like to judge others.

programme.title: The Masterpieces of Ukiyo-e producer Tsutaju
programme.desc: , Episode 2, Season 1. This episode takes a close look at artist Utamaro's iconic "Woman with a Glass Noisemaker.

programme.title: Direct Talk
programme.desc: . He talks about the potential of physical expression. Gene Otani.
programme.category: Episode 255. Leading Japanese dancer and choreographer Kondo Ryohei served as director of the opening ceremony for the Tokyo Deaflympics in November

programme.title: DOSUKOI Sumo Salon 5 minutes
programme.desc: . Only 7% of rikishi make it to the top division. We learn what kind of special perks such top-division rikishi receive, and hear how even some famous Yokozuna had inauspicious beginnings.

programme.title: One in Every Japanese
programme.desc: Japanese have experience as a lay judge in court cases. We witness a networking event for former lay judges, where they discuss unique experiences. What does it feel like to judge others.

programme.title: Ukiyoe Edo-life
programme.desc: . When a little boy's nap is disturbed by a bad dream, his mother comes to his rescue. What was terrorizing him? We examine a print that shows how much the Edo townspeople cherished their kids.

programme.title: Science View
programme.desc: . Squid and cuttlefish use camouflage not just to hide but to communicate. Vanguard Researchers explores the mechanism behind plant immunity. Tomoko Tashiro, Naoko Yamazaki, Michelle Yamamoto, Yoichi Sato, Tomoko Tina Kimura, Rena Yamada, Ross Mihara, Rachel Walzer.

programme.title: Frontrunners
programme.desc: . Taira Yuiko promotes composting of food waste to create a sustainable cycle. She works with an NGO in Mongolia to form a local nutrient cycle, enriching the soil and teaching composting techniques.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia, and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: My Street Piano
programme.desc: . Shimanto is a nature-rich town in Kochi Prefecture. A piano is placed on a park's outdoor stage, and people stop to play from their heart.

programme.title: Trails to Oishii Tokyo
programme.desc: . Wakame, one of Japan's most popular types of seaweed, takes us to Sanriku in Miyagi Prefecture. Experience harvesting giant wakame and explore research advancing environmental protection.

programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia, and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: The World Heritage Sites in Japan
programme.desc: . From natural landscapes to historic architecture representing centuries of cultural development, Japan's World Heritage sites offer a wide range of glimpses into history and the beauty of nature.

programme.title: Japan from Above: Up Close
programme.desc: . This program takes you on an aerial journey across 21st century Japan. Enjoy the bird's-eye view of unique landscapes as well as intimate portraits of the people who inhabit the archipelago.

programme.title: Dive in Tokyo
programme.desc: . Tokyo: where both tradition and the latest trends coexist. Join us on a journey to historic old towns and encounter many fun surprises along the way.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia, and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Century Signboards
programme.desc: , Episode 8, Season 1. An entertainment hall home to rakugo comic stories and more for over 100 years. A place to relax and forget your cares, food and drink are welcome.

programme.title: MATSURI: The Heartbeat of Japan
programme.desc: , Episode 7, Season 1. The highlight of Onabare is nerikomi, which involves the careful wielding of a long, heavy pole. This follows the parade of a portable shrine through the streets with performances along the way.

programme.title: Direct Talk
programme.desc: , Episode 269. Sugiura Mitsuyoshi is gaining attention both at home and abroad for transforming old kimonos into new fashion statements. He talks about his unique efforts to revitalize a traditional industry. Gene Otani.

programme.title: Japan's Top Inventions
programme.desc: , Episode 58. Discover the stories behind renowned Japanese products alongside our reporter. This time: laser flaw inspection systems. Peter Von Gomm, Yuki Rhinehart, Jason David Danielson, Koki Ando, Toru Iwatani, Shigetaka Kurita, Yoshihiko Akimoto.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia, and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Herbal Symphony
programme.desc: , Episode 3, Season 1. In Uda, discover Japan's oldest private medicinal herb garden, where the natural landscape from 300 years ago is preserved in a botanical time capsule, a sanctuary of the local culture and history.

programme.title: Trails to Oishii Tokyo 10min.
programme.desc: Nira, with its garlic-like pungency and ability to boost stamina, is a true star. Visit a production site to marvel at nira's vitality and see how the ingredient can be used in a wide range of dishes.

programme.title: The Garden Way
programme.desc: No Desription Available.

programme.title: Sumopedia
programme.desc: . Sumopedia enriches your sumo experience with insights into the sport’s techniques, traditions and legendary wrestlers. While the rules are simple, this iconic Japanese sport has far more depth than meets the eye.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia, and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: My Street Piano
programme.desc: . Pianos are placed in airports, stations and street corners around the world for anyone to play. Join us as we unfold life stories of each piano player and watch them through fixed camera lenses.

programme.title: A Home for Hope
programme.desc: An NPO offers free tutoring to children born in Japan with foreign roots. More than just a study space, it's a second home where belonging grows and hopes for the future quietly take root.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia, and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: The World Heritage Sites in Japan
programme.desc: . From natural landscapes to historic architecture representing centuries of cultural development, Japan's World Heritage sites offer a wide range of glimpses into history and the beauty of nature.

programme.title: Japan from Above: Up Close
programme.desc: . This program takes you on an aerial journey across 21st century Japan. Enjoy the bird's-eye view of unique landscapes as well as intimate portraits of the people who inhabit the archipelago.

programme.title: Core Kyoto
programme.desc: years ago used cloth dyed with the colors of prayer, many of which are lost to time. Some today are fascinated with these forgotten colors and are attempting to revive them. Gary Tegler, Takuji Takahashi, Ikuko Takeda, Sumie Nagakusa, Tomoko Eri, Toshiaki Nagakusa, Kokei Eri, Yoshiaki Dobuchi.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Century Signboards
programme.desc: . Using traditional wooden barrels, a soy sauce brewer developed its own original flavor, breathing new life into the world of Japanese soy sauce.

programme.title: Satoyama Kitchen Diary
programme.desc: . Chef Nobuko, her cameraman husband Makoto, and their cat Tama take viewers on a tasty journey through local cuisine, served with a side of seasonal scenery. This time, they focus on leopard plant.

programme.title: Japanology Plus
programme.desc: . Sashiko is a type of traditional Japanese embroidery, once vital for the durability and warmth of clothes in cold climates. We explore how the charm of this enduring craft is being rediscovered. Peter Barakan, Kanako Sachno, Mamoru Abe, Hitoshi Nishiyama, Hirotaka Ohata, Sachiko Miyamoto, Kimiko Fukuda, Chiyo Kawahara.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Cycle Around Japan
programme.desc: . A series showcasing the stunning landscapes featured in Cycle Around Japan. This time: the varied, beautiful scenery of Nagasaki. Discover the Goto Islands, and their history of "hidden Christians.". Zac Reynolds.

programme.title: Trails to Oishii Tokyo
programme.desc: . Wakame, one of Japan's most popular types of seaweed, takes us to Sanriku in Miyagi Prefecture. Experience harvesting giant wakame and explore research advancing environmental protection.
